summary: Dideoxynucleotides do not typically serve a role in regular PCR as their primary function is to terminate DNA sequences rather than amplify them; thus, they're excluded due to being unnecessary for typical PCR processes aimed at replication only. The focus should remain strictly on nucleoside triphosphates which enable efficient DNA strand extension required throughout repeated cycles characteristic of successful PCR reactions leading directly toward targeted genetic material duplication goals pursued clinically across various diagnostic applications including disease detection monitoring treatment efficacy assessment among others where
